description_long: This is a heavy-duty splitting maul with a robust, wedge-shaped steel head and a long, slightly curved wooden handle. The head is painted black with a flat poll on one side and a broad, sharp blade on the other, designed for splitting logs. The handle is made of light-colored wood and features a bright red painted grip at the end, enhancing visibility and providing a non-slip surface. The overall construction suggests durability and ergonomic use, with the handle's curvature aiding in powerful swings. The axe head is securely mounted onto the handle, likely using a traditional wedged fitting. This tool is commonly found in urban garages, maintenance sheds, or park service areas for heavy-duty wood splitting tasks.
description: A heavy splitting maul with a black steel wedge head and a long, curved wooden handle featuring a red grip. Designed for splitting logs, it combines durability with ergonomic handling.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the wedge-shaped black steel head with a red grip at the end.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the flat poll of the axe head and the red-painted handle tip, with the handle's width visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the broad blade profile of the axe head.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the wedge geometry of the blade and the secure mounting of the head to the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a red painted grip, oval cross-section, ergonomic for two-handed use.
attribute_blade_design: Wedge-shaped steel blade with a broad splitting edge and a flat poll; single edge, no beard.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fitting securing the steel head to the wooden handle.
